steps: Sign in as the initial super admin using the configured authentication flow.
View a protected application area that is inaccessible to unauthenticated users.
Create or promote another user to an elevated admin role from the admin interface.
Sign in as a non-admin user and confirm restricted admin actions are hidden or blocked.
Sign out and confirm protected routes redirect back to authentication.
Create a new project workspace with a title and basic project metadata.
Invite or add a team member to the project.
Assign a project role to the added team member.
View the team roster for the project and confirm the member appears with the correct role.
Remove or change a team member role and confirm the update is reflected immediately.
Upload a supported source file such as a PDF or document file through the intake interface.
Enter required metadata fields during upload, including source archive, publication, language, and date where available.
Confirm the file is stored successfully and linked to a newly created record.
Confirm the platform preserves the original uploaded file without overwriting it.
Reopen the created record and verify that the uploaded source file and entered metadata are visible.

features.description: Upload Metadata Enforcement and File Naming
steps: Start a file upload and attempt to continue without filling required metadata fields.
Confirm the system blocks completion until required metadata is supplied.
Complete the upload with valid metadata.
Confirm the system applies a consistent generated naming convention to the stored file or record.
Confirm the stored record shows both the original source details and the normalized internal naming outcome.
Create a record through manual upload.
Confirm the record stores the source archive, source identifier or upload source, publication, language, and date if provided.
Confirm the record stores linked file assets separately from text-layer content.
Reopen the record and verify provenance information is still visible and unchanged.
Confirm the record can be updated with additional linked text layers without replacing the original source file.
Query the Ibali integration using a known issue such as an Isigidimi record.
Confirm the platform retrieves issue-level metadata from the Ibali API.
Confirm the platform retrieves or stores linked transcription file references from the Ibali media records.
Confirm extracted transcription text from the Ibali media payload is captured into the record workflow.
Confirm the imported record is saved as a canonical internal record with source provenance marked as Ibali.
Query the NLSA integration using a known collection such as Imvo Zabantsundu.
Confirm the platform retrieves item metadata from the NLSA ContentDM endpoints.
Confirm the platform retrieves or stores the linked source PDF reference for a selected item.
Confirm OCR text exposed by NLSA is captured into the record workflow as a source text layer.
Confirm the imported record is saved as a canonical internal record with source provenance marked as NLSA.
Search or harvest Wits metadata for a Panashe-relevant query such as Imvo or newspaper.
Confirm the platform can store Wits record metadata as a source-linked record or discovery entry.
Where a downloadable file or digital object is publicly available, import or link that file into the record.
Confirm the record is marked as a Wits-derived supplementary source rather than a primary structured newspaper source.
Confirm Wits records can still participate in search and filtering inside the platform.
Open a record that has an attached or linked source file.
View the source file inside the application without downloading it first.
Navigate back to the record metadata without losing context.
Confirm that source provenance remains visible while viewing the source.
Confirm the viewer works for at least one uploaded file and one imported source-linked file.

features.description: OCR and Source Text Acquisition Layer
steps: Open a record imported from a source that already exposes OCR or extracted text.
Confirm the source text is stored as a distinct text layer rather than merged into corrected transcription.
Upload or select a record that requires OCR or text extraction.
Run the text acquisition process and confirm a source text layer is created.
Confirm the record clearly distinguishes the source-acquired text layer from later corrected text layers.
Open a record with at least one source text layer.
Add a second text layer for corrected transcription or corroborating transcription.
Confirm the system stores the two text layers separately.
View both text layers and confirm their labels, provenance, and status are visible.
Confirm updating one text layer does not overwrite the other.
Open a record with a source text layer and enter transcription edit mode.
Save a corrected transcription as a distinct text layer or updated working transcription.
Mark the transcription with a status such as reviewed or needs expert review.
Reopen the record and confirm the corrected transcription persists with its status.
Confirm the original source-acquired text layer is still preserved separately.
Select a record with an eligible transcription layer.
Trigger machine translation draft generation.
Confirm a machine translation text layer is created and linked to the source transcription.
Confirm the machine translation is clearly labeled as a draft.
Confirm the record keeps the translation layer separate from both transcription and annotation layers.
Open a machine translation draft for a record.
Edit the draft and save a corrected translation.
Confirm the corrected translation is stored with author and timestamp context.
Confirm the original machine translation draft remains preserved separately.
Reopen the record and verify both translation versions are still accessible.
Save a corrected translation for a record.
Open another record with the same or closely matching source text segment.
Trigger translation generation or review for that record.
Confirm the system surfaces a prior corrected translation suggestion or reuse alert.
Confirm the user can accept, reject, or edit the suggested reused translation.
Create a protected-term rule for a key concept or term.
Mark the rule with a behavior such as do not translate, always flag, or approved translation.
Open a record containing that term.
Confirm the term is handled according to the configured rule inside the translation workflow.
Confirm the rule can be edited later without deleting the underlying record text.
Open a record that has a source file, transcription, and translation.
View the source file and text layers side by side in the reader.
Confirm a user can switch between or compare available text layers.
Confirm notes or annotations are visible in the same research context.
Confirm the reader remains usable on a standard laptop viewport without breaking layout.
Open a record and add a note or annotation.
Link the note to the relevant record or text-layer context.
Save the note with author attribution.
Reopen the record and confirm the note is still visible.
Edit the note and confirm the updated version persists correctly.
Open a record or text layer and apply an uncertainty or dispute flag.
Save the flagged state.
Reopen the record and confirm the flag is visible in context.
Filter or search for flagged records and confirm the flagged record appears.
Update or clear the flag and confirm the change is reflected.
Search for a known term that exists in at least one imported or uploaded record.
Confirm matching records are returned.
Apply filters such as source archive, language, or date.
Confirm the result set updates correctly when filters are applied.
Open a result and confirm the user lands in the correct record context.
Open a record with source metadata, transcription, and translation.
Trigger citation packet generation.
Confirm the export includes source details, publication, date, and record metadata.
Confirm the export includes the relevant text layers and/or notes.
Confirm the export downloads or opens successfully in the expected format.
Open the corpus trend view for a known term present in multiple records.
Confirm the system groups or visualizes the term across at least one dimension such as date or publication.
Change a filter such as source or language.
Confirm the trend view updates accordingly.
Open one underlying record from the trend view and confirm navigation works.
Open a record that has enough text or metadata to support related suggestions.
View the related records section.
Confirm at least one suggested related record is shown.
Open a suggested record and confirm it is relevant by shared context, term, or source relationship.
Return to the original record and confirm the related suggestions remain available.

features.description: Admin Record Audit and Activity Trace
steps: Perform several actions such as upload, edit, annotation, and translation correction on one or more records.
Open the admin audit or activity view.
Confirm the recent actions appear with user and timestamp context.
Filter or inspect a specific record’s activity history.
Confirm the audit information helps identify who changed what and when.
